notes: Bootstrapped from HY-IG v2 reference pair zoom charts (SL-4, SL-5) plus META-ZI canonical slug registry (dotcom, gfc, covid, taper_2018, inflation_2022). Dot-Com events match the stakeholder sketch from SL-4; GFC events from the NY Fed GFC timeline; COVID events from CBO + Fed H.4.1 launch record; Taper 2018 events from FOMC statements; Inflation 2022 events from FOMC + CPI releases. VIZ-V12 makes this the single source for all event markers on history_zoom_* charts — ad-hoc picks are prohibited. Ray may propose additions (e.g., new episodes aligned with RES-20 pending) via PR; Vera merges. Each entry's rationale + source_citation is mandatory.

dotcom.episode_rationale: Stakeholder sketch SL-4 requested annotated zoom of credit spread behavior around the tech bubble collapse. Reference-pair (HY-IG v2) Story page cites this episode directly.
dotcom.episode_source_citation: NBER US business cycle dating committee; NASDAQ Composite index history.

key_events.label: Mar 2000: Dot-Com peak
key_events.rationale: NASDAQ Composite closed at 5048.62, its all-time peak until 2015. Widely cited as the exact turning point of the tech bubble.
key_events.source_citation: NASDAQ historical close data; Pástor and Veronesi (2006) 'Was There a Nasdaq Bubble in the Late 1990s?' JFE.

key_events.label: Aug 2000: Yield curve inversion
key_events.rationale: 2y-10y Treasury curve fully inverted; historical leading indicator of recession, coinciding with early HY-IG spread widening.
key_events.source_citation: Federal Reserve H.15 daily Treasury rates; Estrella and Trubin (2006) 'The Yield Curve as a Leading Indicator', FRBNY.

key_events.label: Mar 2001: Recession begins
key_events.rationale: NBER-dated start of the 2001 recession; coincides with accelerated HY-IG spread widening as default risk repriced.
key_events.source_citation: NBER US Business Cycle Expansions and Contractions table (March 2001 peak).

key_events.label: Jul 2002: WorldCom bankruptcy
key_events.rationale: Largest US bankruptcy at the time; spread-widening local peak coincides with the WorldCom Chapter 11 filing, marking the episode's credit trough.
key_events.source_citation: SEC Form 8-K WorldCom July 2002 filing; NY Times archive.

gfc.episode_rationale: Stakeholder sketch SL-5 requested annotated zoom of credit spread behavior through the GFC. Reference-pair Story page cites this as the paradigmatic HY-IG spread blowout.
gfc.episode_source_citation: NY Fed Global Financial Crisis Timeline; NBER US business cycle dating.

key_events.label: Aug 2007: BNP fund freeze
key_events.rationale: BNP Paribas suspended three funds citing inability to value subprime holdings — first public rupture of interbank liquidity; HY-IG OAS begins sustained widening.
key_events.source_citation: BNP Paribas press release 2007-08-09; NY Fed GFC Timeline entry.

key_events.label: Mar 2008: Bear Stearns rescue
key_events.rationale: Fed-facilitated JP Morgan acquisition of Bear Stearns; first major broker-dealer failure, HY-IG spreads broke above 600bps for the first time since 2002.
key_events.source_citation: Federal Reserve Board press release 2008-03-16; JP Morgan Chase 8-K.

key_events.label: Sep 2008: Lehman bankruptcy
key_events.rationale: Lehman Brothers Chapter 11 filing; largest bankruptcy in US history; HY-IG spread widened ~450bps in the following 60 days.
key_events.source_citation: Lehman Brothers Chapter 11 petition 2008-09-15; NY Fed GFC Timeline.

key_events.label: Mar 2009: SPX trough
key_events.rationale: S&P 500 intraday low of 666.79; HY-IG OAS peaked at ~1500bps around this window — the canonical credit-equity co-trough.
key_events.source_citation: S&P 500 historical price data; ICE BofA US HY OAS index.

key_events.label: Jun 2009: Recession ends
key_events.rationale: NBER-dated end of the 2007-2009 recession; spreads had normalized to ~800bps, still well above pre-crisis levels but off crisis peak.
key_events.source_citation: NBER US Business Cycle Expansions and Contractions table (June 2009 trough).

covid.episode_rationale: Fastest HY-IG spread blowout in the series' history and fastest Fed response; reference pair Story page uses this episode to illustrate Fed-put speed.
covid.episode_source_citation: NBER February 2020 peak / April 2020 trough dating; Federal Reserve H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances.

key_events.label: Feb 2020: SPX pre-COVID peak
key_events.rationale: S&P 500 closed at 3386.15, the pre-COVID all-time high; marks the start of the drawdown and the kickoff of the HY-IG spread blowout.
key_events.source_citation: S&P 500 historical close data.

key_events.label: Mar 2020: SPX trough + Fed facility
key_events.rationale: S&P 500 closed at 2237.40 low; same day, Fed announced PMCCF / SMCCF corporate credit facilities — fastest Fed put in modern era. HY-IG OAS peaked in the following days at ~1100bps.
key_events.source_citation: Federal Reserve Board press release 2020-03-23; S&P 500 historical data.

key_events.label: Apr 2020: Recession ends
key_events.rationale: NBER-dated end of the COVID recession (shortest on record, 2 months); HY-IG OAS had already collapsed to ~700bps on Fed-backstop effect.
key_events.source_citation: NBER US Business Cycle Expansions and Contractions table (April 2020 trough).

taper_2018.episode_rationale: First post-GFC Fed tightening cycle; Q4 2018 saw the sharpest HY-IG spread widening outside recession, useful for illustrating non-recessionary credit stress. Supports RES-20 (pending Wave 5B-2 Ray dispatch) episode-selection criterion.
taper_2018.episode_source_citation: Federal Reserve FOMC statements 2017-2018; ICE BofA US HY OAS index.

key_events.label: Feb 2018: Volmageddon
key_events.rationale: VIX single-day spike >100% (9.15 to 37.32); SVXY and XIV ETPs blew up. First cross-asset vol shock of the hiking cycle; HY-IG spread widened ~50bps in the following week.
key_events.source_citation: CBOE VIX historical data; XIV termination notice (Credit Suisse 2018-02-06).

key_events.label: Oct 2018: Powell 'long way' remarks
key_events.rationale: Chair Powell stated Fed funds rate was 'a long way from neutral'; triggered risk-off phase that culminated in Q4 equity drawdown and HY-IG spread widening from ~330bps to ~550bps.
key_events.source_citation: Jerome Powell remarks at The Atlantic Festival 2018-10-03; FOMC transcript.

key_events.label: Dec 2018: SPX Christmas Eve low
key_events.rationale: S&P 500 closed down ~19.8% from September peak; HY-IG OAS peaked ~540bps — the widest non-recessionary print of the cycle.
key_events.source_citation: S&P 500 historical price data; ICE BofA US HY OAS index.

key_events.label: Jan 2019: Powell pivot
key_events.rationale: Chair Powell signaled patience on further hikes at AEA panel; HY-IG spreads tightened rapidly in the following quarter — canonical example of rate-policy dovish reversal compressing credit spreads.
key_events.source_citation: Jerome Powell AEA Panel remarks 2019-01-04; FOMC January 2019 statement.

inflation_2022.episode_rationale: Fastest Fed hiking cycle since 1980; HY-IG spreads widened without a recession, useful for stress-testing the signal's regime discrimination under non-recessionary stress. Supports RES-20 (pending Wave 5B-2 Ray dispatch) episode-selection criterion.
inflation_2022.episode_source_citation: BLS CPI releases 2021-2023; Federal Reserve FOMC statements 2022-2023; NBER (no recession dated).

key_events.label: Jan 2022: SPX peak
key_events.rationale: S&P 500 closed at 4796.56, the 2022 pre-drawdown peak; marks the start of the rate-driven equity drawdown and HY-IG spread widening.
key_events.source_citation: S&P 500 historical close data.

key_events.label: Mar 2022: First Fed hike
key_events.rationale: First FOMC rate hike of the cycle (+25bps to 0.25-0.50%); HY-IG spreads began sustained widening from ~340bps.
key_events.source_citation: FOMC statement 2022-03-16.

key_events.label: Jun 2022: CPI 9.1% print
key_events.rationale: BLS May CPI release printed 8.6% YoY (highest since 1981); triggered expectation of 75bps Fed hike and HY-IG spread widening from ~450bps to ~600bps over the following weeks.
key_events.source_citation: BLS CPI release 2022-06-10.

key_events.label: Oct 2022: SPX cycle low
key_events.rationale: S&P 500 closed at 3577.03, cycle low (-25% from January peak); HY-IG OAS peaked ~580bps — non-recessionary credit stress local maximum.
key_events.source_citation: S&P 500 historical price data; ICE BofA US HY OAS index.

key_events.label: Mar 2023: SVB collapse
key_events.rationale: Silicon Valley Bank failure; regional-bank stress caused a 90bps HY-IG OAS widening in one week and Fed emergency BTFP facility launch, but did not tip into recession.
key_events.source_citation: FDIC SVB receivership notice 2023-03-10; Federal Reserve Board BTFP press release 2023-03-12.
